name: Little Spacey
description: This algorithm is a version of the Expert Sleepers VST plug-in of the same name. Little Spacey is delay effect, inspired by classic analogue ‘bucket brigade’ delays but with refinements only possible in the digital arena – not to mention a maximum delay time that would cost a small fortune to realise in hardware. Great care has been taken to give Little Spacey the smooth, creamy sound which makes analogue delays still popular today. In common with the best analogue delays Little Spacey allows you to modulate the delay time for chorus or vibrato effects. Unlike most analogue delays, Little Spacey is not restricted to mono operation, and works well in stereo settings, offering variation of the effect between the channels for particularly rich and involving sounds. Unlike the VST version, this implementation also allow the delay time to be synchronised to clocks (analogue or MIDI).
gui_description: The display simply shows the current delay time, taking into account any clock syncing, and the limits on the delay time imposed by the currently selected BBD size.

specifications.name: Stereo
specifications.description: Whether the algorithm is mono or stereo.

parameters.name: BBD Size
parameters.description: Sets the number of bucket-brigade stages.

parameters.name: Delay time
parameters.description: Sets the delay time, between the minimum and maximum times determined from the BBD size and the current sample rate.

parameters.name: Spread
parameters.description: Sets the difference in delay times between the left and right channels. At 0%, the two channels have the same delay time.

parameters.name: Colour
parameters.description: Allows you to lower the cut-off frequencies of the low pass filters, resulting in delays that lose more of their high frequency components.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.description: Sets the amount of the delayed signal that gets routed back to the input of the delay, causing repeated echoes. Note that the control goes up to 110%. Settings above 100% cause ‘positive feedback’ i.e. the delays get louder over time.

parameters.name: Feedback path
parameters.description: Sets the feedback path, as described above.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.description: The wet/dry mix.

parameters.name: Level
parameters.description: The gain applied to the delay/wet signal.

parameters.name: Dry mix
parameters.description: Controls whether the ‘dry’ output is indeed the true dry sound or a filtered version. The filtered version can be useful when using fairly ‘dark’-sounding delays to match the dry sound more closely to the delayed sound.

parameters.name: LFO 1 Speed
parameters.description: The LFO speed. Exponential scale from 0.05Hz to 20Hz.

parameters.name: LFO 1 Depth
parameters.description: The LFO depth.

parameters.name: LFO 1 Spread
parameters.description: Varies the phase of the LFO applied to each (stereo) channel.

parameters.name: LFO 2 Speed
parameters.description: The LFO speed. Exponential scale from 0.05Hz to 20Hz.

parameters.name: LFO 2 Depth
parameters.description: The LFO depth.

parameters.name: LFO 2 Spread
parameters.description: Varies the phase of the LFO applied to each (stereo) channel.

parameters.name: Ducking
parameters.description: Reduces the level of the delays when the signal level is high. This is typically used to let the initial note ‘cut through’ the delays, with the delays then fading up during the note’s decay tails.

parameters.name: Env->LFO 1 Speed
parameters.description: Sets the amount by which the envelope modifies the modulation speed. Positive settings cause the modulation to speed up when the sound is louder; negative settings cause the modulation to slow down when the sound is louder.

parameters.name: Env->LFO 1 Depth
parameters.description: Sets the amount by which the envelope modifies the modulation depth. Positive settings cause the modulation depth to increase when the sound is louder; negative settings cause the modulation depth to decrease when the sound is louder.

parameters.name: Env->LFO 1 Spread
parameters.description: Sets the amount by which the envelope modifies the modulation LFO’s spread.

parameters.name: Clock source
parameters.description: Chooses the clock source: ‘None’, ‘Clock input’, or ‘MIDI clock’.

parameters.name: MIDI divisor
parameters.description: The MIDI divisor, if the clock source is ‘MIDI clock’.

parameters.name: MIDI numerator
parameters.description: The MIDI numerator, if the clock source is ‘MIDI clock’.

parameters.name: Delay multiplier
parameters.description: A delay multiplier, if the clock source is not ‘None’.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

input_ports.name: Left/mono input
input_ports.description: The left or mono input bus.

input_ports.name: Clock input
input_ports.description: The input bus to use for the clock, if the source is ‘Clock input’.

input_ports.name: Clock input
input_ports.description: The input bus to use for the clock, if the source is ‘Clock input’.

input_ports.name: Left/mono input
input_ports.description: The left or mono input bus.

output_ports.name: Left/mono output
output_ports.description: The left or mono output bus.

output_ports.name: Left/mono output
output_ports.description: The left or mono output bus.
